name: Kraftsman
description_short: Kraftsman Federal Credit Union is a small, member-owned credit union in Hopewell, VA serving manufacturing employees with basic deposit and lending products since 1961.
description_long:
Kraftsman Federal Credit Union was established in 1961 and has operated for 65 years as a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Hopewell, Virginia. The institution serves a specific membership base of manufacturing employees and related workers, reflecting its specialized occupational focus rather than open community membership. With a single branch location at 910 Industrial Street in Hopewell, Kraftsman operates as a small, localized financial institution with modest asset levels.

The credit union offers core financial services typical of small federal credit unions, including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes unsecured personal loans ($201,339 in outstanding balances) and other loans ($1.3 million), suggesting they provide member financing for various purposes. As an NCUA-insured institution, member deposits and share accounts are protected up to federal limits, providing security for depositors.

Kraftsman distinguishes itself through its specialized membership model focused on the manufacturing sector, serving approximately 461 members. This niche approach allows the credit union to tailor services to the needs of manufacturing workers and related professionals. The institution maintains conservative financial practices with total assets of approximately $5 million and total equity of $1.34 million, reflecting a well-capitalized small credit union.

As a very small credit union, Kraftsman offers limited branch access (one location only) and may have restricted service hours or limited product sophistication compared to larger institutions. The member base of 461 suggests limited growth over its 65-year history, and prospective members should verify current eligibility requirements for manufacturing-sector membership. Potential members should contact the credit union directly to understand current membership criteria, product offerings, and any service limitations.
services: Share certificates and other deposit products
Member governance and dividend earnings potential
pros: NCUA-insured member deposits and accounts with federal share insurance protection
Established 65-year track record of service to manufacturing community members
ATM/debit card programs available for member account access
Offers both unsecured and other loan products for member financing needs
Well-capitalized institution with $1.34 million in equity relative to asset size
Non-profit, member-owned structure with governance by members rather than external investors
Direct phone access to local credit union staff at (804) 452-3129
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility to members not near Hopewell, VA
Very small asset base ($5 million) may restrict loan amounts and product variety compared to larger credit unions
Specialized manufacturing-sector membership likely restricts eligibility; not open to general public
No website listed, limiting online information access and digital banking capabilities transparency
Modest member base of 461 suggests limited resources for service expansion or product development
No FHLB membership may restrict funding sources and liquidity management options
best_for: Manufacturing sector employees and related workers living in or near Hopewell, Virginia
Members seeking basic savings, checking, and small loan products from a stable local institution
Individuals who qualify for occupational membership and prefer member-owned credit union structure over for-profit banks
diagnosis: Kraftsman Federal Credit Union is best suited for manufacturing sector employees and related workers in the Hopewell, Virginia area seeking basic financial services from a stable, member-owned institution. The primary caveat is its restricted membership (manufacturing-sector only), single branch location, and very small size, which limits service options and accessibility compared to larger credit unions or banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
